0.description: The **request capture rate** shows captured requests as a percentage of all transactions processed by OneAgent monitored applications or hosts. The **trace capture rate** shows captured traces as a percentage of all observed end-to-end transactions. Because a single trace can contain multiple requests, the trace capture rate can be lower than the request capture rate.

2.title: Full-Stack OneAgent capture rates
2.description:
The **request capture rate** represents the ratio between captured requests and the total number of transactions processed by **OneAgent** monitored application or host. 

In this chart, the blue line shows the **trace capture rate** and the red line shows the **request capture rate** over time. The metrics require at least OneAgent version 1.305.

leftYAxisSettings.label: Oneagent - Code Module - Requests - Sampled • Oneagent - Code Module - Requests - Processed • Oneagent - Code Module - Traces - Sampled • Oneagent - Code Module - Traces - Processed

3.title: Full-Stack trace data volume
3.description:
Amount of trace data ingested from Full-Stack monitored applications or hosts. The chart includes

* The trace data volume captured by OneAgent and regulated by Adaptive Traffic Management (green bars).
* The Full-Stack included trace volume based on the contributing Full-Stack memory-gibibytes (blue line).
* The trace data volume ingested from Full-Stack monitored applications or hosts but not regulated by Adaptive Traffic Management (`fullstack-fixed-rate-ingested_bytes_sum`; blue bar). This includes OpenTelemetry spans and other fixed-rate traffic and can exceed the included limit; [the excess will be charged](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/dps-full-stack#full-stack-traces).

leftYAxisSettings.label: (DPS) Maximum included Full-Stack trace volume • (DPS) Maximum configured Full-Stack trace volume

4.title: Full-Stack trace volume
4.description:
Dynatrace ingests trace data from multiple sources, which are licensed differently.

* `fullstack-adaptive` shows trace data captured by OneAgent on Full-Stack monitored hosts and applications and regulated by Adaptive Traffic Management.
* `fullstack-fixed-rate` shows trace data from Full-Stack monitored sources that use fixed-rate sampling (for example, OpenTelemetry spans or fixed-rate OneAgent settings). This traffic still consumes the Full-Stack included trace volume but is not automatically adjusted by Adaptive Traffic Management and can exceed the included limit, with the excess billed as Traces – Ingest & Process.
* Other series (for example, serverless or OTLP-only sources) represent trace data that is not part of Full-Stack Monitoring and is not controlled by Adaptive Traffic Management.

On this chart, green bars represent `fullstack-adaptive`, blue bars represent `fullstack-fixed-rate`, magenta bars represent `otlp-trace-ingest`, and red bars represent `serverless` trace data.
4.query:
fetch dt.system.events
| filter event.type == "Traces - Ingest & Process"
| filter licensing_type == "fullstack-adaptive" or licensing_type == "fullstack-fixed-rate" 
| makeTimeseries ingested_bytes_sum = sum(ingested_bytes), time: usage.start, interval:1h, by: {licensing_type}, nonempty:true

10.title: Full-Stack trace volume used
10.description:
Ingested trace volume, as a percentage of your licensed Full-Stack included trace volume. Adaptive Traffic Management keeps it around the Full-Stack included limit. The algorithm used in Dynatrace accounts for a degree of fluctuation, allowing the used trace volume to exceed 100% without extra charges

* This can exceed 100% if you opted for [Extended trace ingest on top of Full-Stack Monitoring](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/dps-full-stack#full-stack-traces) , this excess will be charged.
* This can exceed 100% if you sent OpenTelemetry traces or other fixed rate span data via API from Full-Stack monitored sources, this excess will be charged.

leftYAxisSettings.label: (DPS) Maximum included Full-Stack trace volume • (DPS) Maximum configured Full-Stack trace volume

11.title: Average size of Full-Stack spans
11.description: Average size of spans ingested from Full-Stack monitored applications or hosts. Typical values are in the 1.5-2 KiB range; if the span size is larger and the used trace volume is high (or the trace capture rate is low), you might be capturing a lot of data per span. In this chart, the green line shows spans from adaptive Full-Stack trace ingest (`fullstack-adaptive`), and the blue line shows spans from fixed-rate Full-Stack trace ingest (`fullstack-fixed-rate`).

leftYAxisSettings.label: (DPS) Maximum included Full-Stack trace volume • (DPS) Maximum configured Full-Stack trace volume

12.title: Contributing Full-Stack memory-gibibyte
12.description: Contributing Full-Stack memory-gibibytes from monitored hosts and applications. The blue line (`contributing_gib`) is derived from `dt.billing.full_stack_monitoring.usage` and normalized to represent contributing GiB per hour, matching the DPS **Full-Stack Monitoring billing usage**. This value is used to calculate your Full-Stack included trace volume (200 KiB of trace data per minute, or 3000 KiB per 15-minute interval, for each contributing GiB).
12.query:
timeseries contributing_gib = sum(dt.billing.full_stack_monitoring.usage), interval:15m, nonempty:true
| fieldsAdd intervalInMinutes = toLong(interval) / 60000000000
| fieldsAdd contributing_gib = contributing_gib[] * (60 / intervalInMinutes)

17.title: Adaptive trace volume per contributing memory-gibibytes per minute
17.description: Average adaptive trace volume every 15 minutes (`trace_volume_per_gibh`; green area). Full-Stack Monitoring starts from 200 KiB/min of trace volume per contributing GiB (3000 KiB per 15-minute interval), which is highlighted by the threshold line in the chart.

18.title: Fixed rate trace volume per contributing memory-gibibytes per minute
18.description: Average fixed-rate trace volume every 15 minutes for fixed-rate Full-Stack traces (`trace_volume_per_gibh`; blue area). This helps you compare fixed-rate trace volume per contributing GiB with the default 200 KiB/min (3000 KiB per 15-minute interval) included with Full-Stack Monitoring; thresholds highlight when volume per GiB approaches or exceeds this level.

21.title: Full-Stack trace ingest and billable extended ingest
21.description: The relationship between the amount of ingested trace data (`included_ingested_byte_sum`; green bar) and the Full-Stack included trace volume (`included_limit`; blue line). If you opted for Extended trace ingest for Full-Stack Monitoring, Adaptive Traffic Management adjusts trace ingest against the configured limit (`configured_limit`; red line), and the extended trace volume charged via Traces – Ingest & Process is shown as `billingAmount` (orange bar).

leftYAxisSettings.label: (DPS) Maximum included Full-Stack trace volume • (DPS) Maximum configured Full-Stack trace volume

27.content: ### Extended trace ingest for Full-Stack Monitoring

28.title: Full-Stack extended trace ingest calculator
28.description:
Use this chart to simulate Extended trace ingest for Full-Stack Monitoring. Set the `ExtraIngestFactor` dashboard variable to specify how many times above the Full-Stack included trace volume you want to configure. The chart shows the Full-Stack included limit (`included_limit`; blue line), the predicted configured limit (`predicted_configured_limit`; red line), the trace volume covered by the included limit (`included_ingested_byte_sum`; green bar), and the predicted billable extended ingest (`predicted_billing_amount`; orange bar).

The current ExtraIngestFactor is $ExtraIngestFactor.
28.query:
// 1. Fetch the necessary data
fetch dt.system.events

// 2. Filter the data out
| filter event.type == "Traces - Ingest & Process"
| filter licensing_type == "fullstack-adaptive" 

// 3. Make a timeseries out of the remaining data
| makeTimeseries {ingested_bytes_sum = sum(ingested_bytes)}, time: usage.start, interval:15m, nonempty:true

// 4. Join a timeseries
| join [
  timeseries { 
    included_limit_min = avg(dt.billing.traces.maximum_included_fullstack_volume_per_minute), 
    service_calls_processed = sum(dt.sfm.oneagent.service_calls.processed), 
    service_calls_received = sum(dt.sfm.server.service_calls.received)
  }, interval:15m, nonempty:true
  | fieldsAdd intervalInMinutes = toLong(interval) / 60000000000
  | fieldsAdd captureRate= service_calls_received[]/service_calls_processed[]
  | fieldsAdd included_limit = included_limit_min[] * intervalInMinutes
  | fieldsAdd predicted_configured_limit = included_limit_min[] * intervalInMinutes * toDouble($ExtraIngestFactor)
] , kind:outer, on:{timeframe}, fields: {included_limit,predicted_configured_limit,captureRate}

// 5. Add computed fields to the joined timeseries
| fieldsAdd maximum_potential_ingestable_trace_data = ingested_bytes_sum[] / captureRate[]
| fieldsAdd min_overage = ingested_bytes_sum[] - included_limit[]
| fieldsAdd min_overage = if(min_overage[] > 0, min_overage[], else: 0)
| fieldsAdd extra_ingest_volume = predicted_configured_limit[] - included_limit[]
| fieldsAdd predicted_billing_amount = maximum_potential_ingestable_trace_data[] - included_limit[]
| fieldsAdd predicted_billing_amount = if(predicted_billing_amount[] > 0, predicted_billing_amount[], else: 0)
| fieldsAdd predicted_billing_amount = if(predicted_billing_amount[] > extra_ingest_volume[], extra_ingest_volume[], else: predicted_billing_amount[])
| fieldsAdd predicted_billing_amount = if (min_overage[] > predicted_billing_amount[], min_overage[], else: predicted_billing_amount[])
| fieldsAdd predicted_billing_amount = if(toDouble($ExtraIngestFactor) > 1.0, predicted_billing_amount[], else: 0)
| fieldsAdd included_ingested_byte_sum = if (predicted_billing_amount[] > 0,included_limit[], else: ingested_bytes_sum[])
| fieldsAdd predicted_capture_rate = if(((included_ingested_byte_sum[]+predicted_billing_amount[])/maximum_potential_ingestable_trace_data[]*100)<100,
                                         (included_ingested_byte_sum[]+predicted_billing_amount[])/maximum_potential_ingestable_trace_data[]*100,else:100)
| fields predicted_configured_limit, included_limit, included_ingested_byte_sum, predicted_capture_rate, predicted_billing_amount, timeframe, interval

leftYAxisSettings.label: (DPS) Maximum included Full-Stack trace volume • (DPS) Maximum configured Full-Stack trace volume

29.title: Predicted extended ingest billable amount
29.description: Total predicted Extended trace ingest that will be billed for the selected timeframe, based on the configured `ExtraIngestFactor`.

30.content:
### Full-Stack Adaptive Traffic Management and trace capture
The Full-Stack included trace volume scales with host or application memory. [Adaptive Traffic Management](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/adaptive-traffic-management-saas-dps) intelligently samples traces in Full-Stack Monitoring. OneAgent adjusts sampling to keep ingested volume within your total trace volume.

Use trace ingest control and sampling settings to prioritize critical requests (eg. HTTP/RPC) and allocate volume between Adaptive Traffic Management and fixed-rate spans.

If you need more trace data, please request extended trace ingest for Full-Stack Monitoring. Learn more in the [licensing documentation](https://docs.dynatrace.com/docs/shortlink/dps-full-stack#full-stack-traces).
